content.text: Root cause: `flb_mp_accessor_keys_remove()` used `mpa->matches` as a mutable scratch buffer. In Loki output, the `remove_mpa` accessor is stored in the shared plugin context and is used concurrently when `Workers > 1`, so multiple threads raced on `mpa->matches`, leading to random/partial key removals. Fix: - Made `flb_mp_accessor_keys_remove()` allocate a per-call `matches_table` scratch array (instead of mutating `mpa->matches`), and updated helper functions (`accessor_key_find_matches`, `accessor_sub_pack`) to use that per-call table. Tests: - I couldn’t add a standalone multithreaded C regression test without wiring it into the repo’s CMake (manual compilation failed due to additional internal include requirements). - Existing Loki runtime tests still pass after the change: - `/testbed/build/bin/flb-rt-out_loki` (all OK) No new test files were left behind (any temporary test attempt was deleted).

trajectory.result: Root cause: `flb_mp_accessor_keys_remove()` used `mpa->matches` as a mutable scratch buffer. In Loki output, the `remove_mpa` accessor is stored in the shared plugin context and is used concurrently when `Workers > 1`, so multiple threads raced on `mpa->matches`, leading to random/partial key removals. Fix: - Made `flb_mp_accessor_keys_remove()` allocate a per-call `matches_table` scratch array (instead of mutating `mpa->matches`), and updated helper functions (`accessor_key_find_matches`, `accessor_sub_pack`) to use that per-call table. Tests: - I couldn’t add a standalone multithreaded C regression test without wiring it into the repo’s CMake (manual compilation failed due to additional internal include requirements). - Existing Loki runtime tests still pass after the change: - `/testbed/build/bin/flb-rt-out_loki` (all OK) No new test files were left behind (any temporary test attempt was deleted).
